accordion_sections.heading: Assessment
accordion_sections.html:
• Confirm menopausal status at first assessment.
  • Take focused symptom history including acute severe pain (torsion/rupture risk), bloating/fullness, appetite change and urinary symptoms.
  • Perform abdominal and (where appropriate) pelvic examination, checking for mass, tenderness/peritonism, distension and ascites features.
  • Request transvaginal ultrasound first line (or transabdominal if TVUSS not possible) and classify morphology/size.
  • Postmenopausal: check CA125 for any ovarian mass and calculate RMI.
  • Premenopausal: reserve CA125 for complex/suspicious masses; do not use routinely for clearly simple cysts.

accordion_sections.heading: Primary care management
accordion_sections.html:
• If emergency features are present, arrange same-day hospital assessment.
  • Premenopausal simple cyst <5 cm: no routine follow-up.
  • Premenopausal simple cyst 5-7 cm: interval ultrasound follow-up (typically 3-6 months).
  • Premenopausal cyst >7 cm, persistent or enlarging: refer to gynaecology.
  • Postmenopausal simple unilateral unilocular cyst ≤3 cm: no routine follow-up.
  • Postmenopausal simple cyst >3 to <5 cm with normal CA125 and no suspicious features: conservative management with interval follow-up.
  • Postmenopausal cyst >5 cm, persistent/symptomatic, abnormal CA125 or suspicious morphology: refer.
  • Any complex cyst: refer based on urgency criteria.

accordion_sections.heading: Who to refer
accordion_sections.html:
Hospital admission (same day)

  • Suspected ovarian torsion.
  • Suspected ruptured/haemorrhagic cyst with acute abdomen.
  • Haemodynamic instability, sepsis concern, or uncontrolled acute pain.

Urgent suspicion of cancer (USOC)

  • Postmenopausal RMI >200.
  • Suspicious malignant USS features (solid areas/papillary projections, ascites, bilateral complex mass, metastatic features).
  • Clinical picture strongly suspicious for ovarian malignancy.

Urgent (non-cancer outpatient)

  • Significant symptomatic complex cyst not yet meeting USOC threshold.
  • Concerning persistent symptoms requiring rapid specialist review.

Routine (outpatient)

  • Premenopausal simple cyst >7 cm, or persistent/enlarging cyst.
  • Postmenopausal cyst >5 cm or persistent cyst requiring specialist management.
  • Persistent symptoms with indeterminate non-USOC imaging.

Include in referral

  • Menopausal status and symptom timeline.
  • Ultrasound morphology, size and laterality.
  • CA125 and RMI (postmenopausal).
  • Relevant family history and prior imaging trajectory.

accordion_sections.heading: Who not to refer
accordion_sections.html:
• Premenopausal simple cysts <5 cm in otherwise well patients.
  • Postmenopausal simple unilateral unilocular cysts ≤3 cm with no concerning features.
  • Selected postmenopausal simple cysts >3 to <5 cm with normal CA125 and stable interval imaging.

accordion_sections.heading: References
accordion_sections.html:
1. RCOG Green-top Guideline No.34: Ovarian cysts in postmenopausal women.
  2. RCOG Green-top 34 update page (Dec 2025 amendment).
  3. Right Decisions NHS Borders: Ovarian cysts/masses management.
